articles.title: José Luis Daza destacó un "giro de 180 grados" en la imagen global de la Argentina tras reuniones en Washington
articles.content: El viceministro aseguró que el país pasó de ser un problema a un ejemplo ante el FMI y el Banco Mundial, con mejoras en financiamiento y expectativas económicas.

articles.title: Desigualdad en los ingresos: advierten que hay argentinos que viven con sólo 8 dólares por día
articles.content: El estudio señala que estas diferencias no solo reflejan disparidades económicas, si no que también impactan de manera directa en el acceso a bienes y servicios esenciales como la alimentación, la salud y la educación.

articles.title: No gastes todos tus dólares: la guía definitiva para visitar Nueva York con un presupuesto ajustado
articles.content: La Gran Manzana puede ser muy accesible si conocés ciertos trucos para recorrerla sin que el bolsillo sufra más de la cuenta.

articles.title: La billetera virtual que ofrece 50% de reintegro en el recargo de la tarjeta SUBE
articles.content: Conocé todos los descuentos y promociones que ofrece la aplicación de esta entidad bancaria para el mes de abril.

articles.title: Las inundaciones arrasaron la producción hortícola en Santa Fe y anticipan suba de precios
articles.content: Las intensas lluvias provocaron pérdidas de hasta el 90% en el cinturón frutihortícola del Gran Santa Fe. Productores advierten faltantes y aumentos en verduras de hoja en la región.

articles.title: Cuánto salen las bolsas de vacío para ropa: las alternativas para guardar las prendas de verano
articles.content: Con la llegada del frío, las prendas para el calor empiezan a ocupar lugar y necesitan ser guardadas de la mejor manera para la siguiente temporada.

articles.title: Cambio en el mapa del empleo: crece en provincias energéticas mientras la industria pierde casi 40.000 puestos
articles.content: El modelo económico apunta a sectores exportadores como petróleo, gas y minería, pero el empleo formal sigue cayendo en ramas industriales y grandes distritos urbanos.

articles.title: El 57% de los argentinos ya usa inteligencia artificial en el trabajo, mientras crece el temor al reemplazo laboral
articles.content: Ya el 57% de los trabajadores usa IA en sus tareas diarias, según Bumeran. Sin embargo, cuatro de cada diez creen que la tecnología reemplazará puestos laborales en el corto plazo.

articles.title: Argentina sentiría el impacto global de la guerra en Medio Oriente: Oxford Economics recortó la previsión de crecimiento
articles.content: Un informe advirtió que el conflicto internacional ya presiona sobre combustibles, subsidios e ingresos reales. El país crecería 2,4% en 2026, pero con una inflación cercana al 30%, por encima de lo previsto por el FMI.

articles.title: Acuerdo con el FMI: qué implica el "salvavidas" de u$s4.700 millones y cómo impactará en la inflación y el dólar
articles.content: El nuevo entendimiento incluye menor presión fiscal, asistencia financiera y un diagnóstico más cauteloso sobre la evolución de la economía.

articles.title: Indian Stocks’ Rally Faces Test as Oil Jumps on Renewed US-Iran Tensions
articles.content: Nifty capped a second straight weekly advance on Friday.

articles.title: Gold Falls as Renewed Hormuz Disruption Stokes Inflation Concern
articles.content: Gold fell after a weekend flare-up in Middle Eastern waters renewed inflation risks from an energy-supply shock and cast doubt over talks to end the war.

articles.title: China’s Government Urges ‘Every Effort’ to Curb Solar Capacity
articles.content: Chinese authorities have called for “every effort” to strengthen capacity controls in the solar industry, as the sector continues to struggle with excess production.

articles.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Erase War Losses on AI Optimism
articles.content: Emerging-market equities fully recovered their losses from the Iran war, as optimism over artificial intelligence trades lifted Asian stocks.

articles.title: Former US Ambassador: Trump May End Iran Ceasefire Soon
articles.content: Former US Ambassador and Washington Institute Distinguished Fellow James Jeffrey says US-Iran negotiations remain fragile after a chaotic weekend, with a US team planning to travel to Islamabad, likely Wednesday, for a second round of talks. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Oil and Gas Jump After US Seizure of Iranian Ship Imperils Talks
articles.content: Oil and natural gas prices soared after the US Navy seized an Iranian ship during a chaotic weekend that saw Tehran firing at vessels and reimposing controls in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: US Seizes Iranian Ship in Blockade
articles.content: Tensions in the Iran war ratcheted up over the weekend as the US Navy fired upon and boarded an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman. It’s the first seizure in the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: China to Form Brokerage With $86 Billion Assets by Merging Firms
articles.content: Two state-backed brokerages in Shanghai have unveiled plans to merge, creating a firm with $86 billion of assets and advancing China’s ambition to forge investment banks capable of competing with Wall Street’s elite.

articles.title: Oil Jumps on Renewed Iran Tensions, Dollar Gains: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Oil jumped and the dollar strengthened as a relief rally driven by hopes of easing strains in the Middle East unraveled, giving way to renewed caution after a weekend flare-up in US-Iran tensions.

articles.title: Korean Stocks Erase War Loss as Chipmakers Rise on AI Resurgence
articles.content: South Korean stocks have erased the slide triggered by the Iran war, driven by a rally in chipmakers as the artificial intelligence trade swings back into investor focus.

articles.title: Oil Jumps on Iran Tensions, US Stock Futures Drop: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Oil jumped and US equity-index futures retreated as traders turned cautious following a weekend flare-up in US-Iran tensions, curbing optimism that strains in the Middle East were easing.

articles.title: AI’s Token Economy Revolution Creates New China Tech Winners
articles.content: China’s cheap artificial intelligence models are rapidly attracting global users and creating new winners in the nation’s stock market.

articles.title: Emerging-Market Stocks Erase War Losses on AI Demand, Peace Hope
articles.content: Emerging-market shares recouped their Iran war-induced losses, lifted by a rebound in Asian equities driven by optimism about artificial intelligence and lingering hopes for fresh Middle East tensions to ease.

articles.title: China to Begin Special Bond Sales With Record 30-Year Offering
articles.content: China will kick off this year’s ultra-long special government bond sales with a record offering of 30-year notes on Friday, in a move that will offer a gauge of investor demand.

articles.title: Major Japanese Life Insurer to Slow Buying of Domestic Debt
articles.content: Fukoku Mutual Life Insurance Co. plans to slow purchases of Japanese government debt this fiscal year as it sees limited upside in super-long bond yields.

articles.title: Oil and Gas Jump After US Seizure of Iranian Ship Imperils Talks
articles.content: Oil and natural gas prices have soared after the US Navy seized an Iranian-flagged cargo ship during a chaotic weekend that saw Tehran firing at vessels and reimposing controls in the Strait of Hormuz. Bloomberg's Laura Davison and Stephen Stapczynski report on the latest. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: La tregua rinde más que los misiles: Irán ofrece reabrir Ormuz, el petróleo descomprime y Wall Street barre con los récords
articles.content: Donald Trump mantuvo el bloqueo naval sobre los puertos iraníes y Teherán respondió cerrando nuevamente el estrecho de Ormuz. Su bloqueo permanente detonó una crisis de la energía y se convirtió en la principal hipoteca del conflicto.

articles.title: Crece el debate en la city por la inflación de los próximos meses, mientras el Gobierno busca blindar los pagos de la deuda
articles.content: Tras el mal dato de inflación y dudas sobre la velocidad de su desaceleración, el Gobierno busca fortalecer la confianza financiera al empezar a asegurar los pagos de deuda de julio, con respaldo de organismos internacionales. La semana traerá además datos clave de actividad, balanza comercial y la mora, en un contexto todavía condicionado por la tensión global.

articles.title: European Natural Gas Rises as Iran Closes Strait of Hormuz Again
articles.content: European natural gas rose after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz shortly after reopening the waterway, choking the flow of energy from the Persian Gulf.

articles.title: The China Show 4/20/2026 (Video)
Humanoid Robot Beats Human Record in Beijing
articles.content: Bloomberg's Minmin Low highlights a half marathon held in Beijing, where autonomous robots showcased significant technological advancements including beating a human record. The event showcases China's rapid advances in robotics and artificial intelligence. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: AirAsia Tests Market With Deutsche-Led $230 Million Private Debt
articles.content: Deutsche Bank AG is marketing a $230 million private-credit deal for Malaysian budget airline AirAsia Aviation Group, according to people familiar with the matter, testing investor demand for the carrier amid rising fuel prices.

articles.title: JPMorgan Sees Year‑Long Path Back to $75 Brent
articles.content: Parsley Ong, Asia Energy & Chemicals Research Head at JPMorgan, explains the scenarios her team has mapped out for oil prices as tensions continue to simmer in the Strait of Hormuz. She speaks on Bloomberg's The China Show. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Taiwan Weighs US Dollar Dividend Payouts for Listed Companies
articles.content: Taiwan is weighing a proposal to permit listed companies to distribute dividends in US dollars, a change from the rule requiring payouts in the local currency, according to people familiar with the matter.

(Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Stocks Set to Fall on Renewed US-Iran Tensions

articles.content: Copper eased from its highest close since early February, as peace talks between Washington and Tehran were thrown into uncertainty after the US seized an Iranian ship in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: Investors Bet ‘Peak Uncertainty’ Has Passed as Asia Stocks Climb
articles.content: Asian stocks rose Monday as investors looked past renewed Middle East tensions over the weekend, shifting focus back to fundamentals and the prospect of further talks.

articles.title: Zimbabwe’s Currency Is Undervalued by Nearly Half, Governor Says
articles.content: Zimbabwe’s currency is undervalued by almost half, the governor of the central bank said, pointing to the foreign reserves and gold that back the unit.

articles.title: Iran War Deepens China’s Dependence on the US for Niche Gas
articles.content: China is set to import a record volume of US ethane this month as petrochemical producers seek alternative feedstocks for their operations after the war in the Middle East choked off crucial supplies.

articles.title: US Seizes Iranian Ship, Peace Talks in Doubt
articles.content:
Over the weekend, the US Navy fired upon and boarded an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman. It's the first seizure in the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

President Donald Trump and Iranian officials offered disparate views on the next stage of the war, casting uncertainty over whether the two sides would meet for peace talks with a ceasefire set to expire in the coming days. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Iron Ore Gains With China Demand Resilient Ahead of May Day
articles.content: Iron ore rose as Chinese demand remained strong ahead of the May Day holidays while near-term supply was constrained.

articles.title: Horizons Middle East & Africa 4/20/2026 (Video)
Hedge Funds Turn Bullish on Cotton for First Time in Two Years
articles.content: Money managers have turned net-bullish on cotton as surging oil prices tied to the war in Iran boost the appeal of natural fiber over increasingly expensive synthetics.

articles.title: How Niche Insurance Shielded Bad Bunny From Bad Weather
articles.content: Bad Bunny had a problem. Days before the Puerto Rican mega-star was set to perform in Colombia earlier this year, the possibility of heavy rains threatened to turn three sold-out shows in Medellin into multimillion-dollar losses.

articles.title: Has the U.S. Seizure of an Iranian Ship Doomed Peace Talks? | Insight with Haslinda Amin 04/20/2026
articles.content: Insight with Haslinda Amin, a daily news program featuring in-depth, high-profile interviews and analysis to give viewers the complete picture on the stories that matter. The show features prominent leaders spanning the worlds of business, finance, politics and culture. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: A Stock Trader’s Guide to a Fractured Economic World
articles.content: Investors are set to pour more money into defense, energy and technology stocks as the Middle East war forces governments to prioritize security and become more self reliant.

articles.title: Ninety One Sees South Africa Opportunities in War Volatility
articles.content: Ninety One Plc remains constructive on South African equities, saying the war in Iran has created opportunities to buy stocks at prices that are lower than justified by their earnings outlook.

articles.title: Revolut CEO Storonsky Says Digital Bank’s IPO Is Two Years Out
articles.content: Revolut Ltd. Chief Executive Officer Nik Storonsky said he wants to take the London-based digital bank public, but not until at least 2028, extending the timeline on one of Europe’s most anticipated offerings.

articles.title: China’s Rare Earth Exports to Japan Show Marked Drop in March
articles.content: China’s exports of rare-earth magnets and materials to Japan fell sharply in March, as souring relations between the Asian nations heighten concerns about a potential supply squeeze.

articles.title: Saxo Sees 'Confusion' Reigning in Oil Markets
articles.content: Ole Hansen, commodity strategy head at Saxo Bank, discusses market sentiment as oil jumped amid a weekend flare-up in US-Iran tensions. "Even with the setback we saw on Friday, these prices are still too high relative to what potential consumers are prepared to pay," Hansen tells Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: China’s Silver Imports Jump to Record on Retail and Solar Demand
articles.content: China’s imports of silver surged to an all-time high in March as demand from retail investors and the country’s massive solar industry pushed purchases well above the seasonal average.

articles.title: Iran War: US Seize Iranian Ship & Peace Talks Uncertain | Daybreak Europe 4/20/2026
articles.content:
Bloomberg Daybreak Europe is your essential morning viewing to stay ahead. Live from London, we set the agenda for your day, catching you up with overnight markets news from the US and Asia. 
                                                                                                                                                                                                      Tensions in the Iran war ratcheted up over the weekend as the US Navy fired upon and boarded an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman, the first seizure in the US blockade of the Strait of Hormuz.

President Donald Trump and Iranian officials offered disparate views on the next stage of the war, casting uncertainty over whether the two sides would meet for peace talks in Pakistan with a ceasefire set to expire on Tuesday.

Rising uncertainty over Iran peace prospects drove up oil and the dollar as US equity futures declined. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: William Hill Owner Evoke in Takeover Talks With Bally’s Intralot
articles.content: Bally’s Intralot SA is in talks to buy William Hill owner Evoke Plc in a proposed deal that could rescue the struggling British gambling firm.

articles.title: Oil rebounds as Iran threatens to retaliate for US attack on cargo ship
articles.content: Hopes for peace deal fade amid uncertainty over possible talks in Pakistan this week

articles.title: Out of office, still hyper-responsive
articles.content: Once established by professional training, the addictive pull of screens, newsfeeds and email replies is impossible to shake

articles.title: Hedge Funds Get 'Gazumped' in New Poaching Strategy
articles.content: The fight for talent in the hedge-fund industry has reached new extremes, with hedge funders using maneuvers like "gazumping" to grab more money for themselves, resulting in a vicious price spiral. Just how much gazumping is going on is unclear, but recruiters say some traders now are securing pay packages of $50 million or more. Clients end up footing the bill for these practices through opaque "passthrough" fees. Bloomberg's Tom Metcalf reports. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Dollar Will Continue to Fall in Next Few Months, Barclays Says
articles.content: Mitul Kotecha, head of foreign exchange and macro strategy Asia at Barclays, discusses the outlook for the dollar. "If our base case of a de-escalation does occur, and we do see a continued de-escalation, we see some sort of move towards a peace deal and an off ramp, then that should continue to put pressure on the dollar," Kotecha tells Bloomberg Television. "But this is a near-term view in the next few months," he adds. "Ultimately, we still like the dollar." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Emerging-Market Bond Sales Are Soaring Again as Investors Dive Back Into Risk
articles.content: Emerging-market bond sales are roaring back from last month’s doldrums, as issuers from Brazil to Turkey take advantage of rebounding markets to raise fresh financing.

articles.title: William Hill Owner in Takeover Talks With Bally’s Intralot
articles.content: Bally’s Intralot SA is in talks to buy William Hill owner Evoke Plc in a proposed deal that could rescue the struggling British gambling firm.

articles.content: Honeywell International Inc. is nearing a deal to divest its productivity solutions and services business to industrial manufacturer Brady Corp., according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Drop in UK Bond Sales to Limit Political Risk Selloff, Citi Says
articles.content: A drop in the UK’s planned bond sales may give the debt some support to counter a selloff driven by political risks, according to Citigroup Inc.

articles.title: Pernod Ricard Is Said to Begin IPO Process for India Unit
articles.content: Pernod Ricard SA, the maker of Absolut vodka and Chivas Regal whisky, has begun work on a potential initial public offering of its Indian business, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: UAE, US Discuss a Potential Financial Lifeline: WSJ
articles.content: The United Arab Emirates has reportedly opened talks with the US about a financial backstop in case the country plunges into further crisis due to the war on Iran, according to the Wall Street Journal. Kasia Klimasinska reports on Bloomberg Television. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Africa’s Oil Shock Was Preventable
articles.content: Africa’s oil wealth hasn’t translated into fuel security, leaving the continent exposed to shocks like the Iran war, explains Bloomberg Opinion columnist Justice Malala. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.content: Dovish comments could fuel another leg down in Treasury yields.

articles.content: Se incorporan nuevas pautas para presentar la declaración jurada simplificada y se unifican los procedimientos con el esquema general.

articles.title: US Premarket Movers for April 20, 2026

articles.content: Oil jumped, driving US stocks and Treasuries lower after a turbulent weekend in the Middle East cast doubt on the prospects for peace talks ahead of a looming ceasefire deadline.

articles.title: Wall Street Strategists Bullish on the US Earnings Outlook
articles.content: Strategists at some of Wall Street’s biggest banks are upbeat on the outlook for US earnings after a positive start to the first-quarter reporting season.

articles.title: Broker Groww’s Profit Tops Estimates After 100% Post-IPO Surge
articles.content: Billionbrains Garage Ventures Ltd. reported fourth-quarter profit that was above estimates, as investors seek growth to justify a 100% jump in the stock since its initial public offering — a surge that has made it the world’s most expensive brokerage.

articles.title: Honeywell to Sell Productivity Business in $1.4 Billion Deal
articles.content: Honeywell International Inc. agreed to divest its productivity solutions and services business to industrial manufacturer Brady Corp., the latest move by Honeywell to reshape its portfolio as it pursues a multipart breakup.

articles.title: Chaos in Hormuz Should Be a Reality Check for Oil Prices
articles.content: Commodity flows from the Persian Gulf are back at a near-standstill, a reminder of the intractable nature of the war in Iran.

articles.title: Stocks Fall, Oil Rises After US Seizes Iran-Flagged Ship | Bloomberg Brief 4/20/2026
articles.content: US equity futures fall and oil climbs after the US Navy attacked and boarded an Iranian-flagged cargo ship in the Gulf of Oman. Iran says it has no plans to attend potential negotiations with the US in Pakistan with the ceasefire expiring on Tuesday. Amy Wu Silverman of RBC Capital Markets discusses the market volatility amid renewed uncertainties around the war.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Energy Services Set to Benefit Once Iran War Ends, Barclays Says
articles.content: The European energy services sector faces a brighter future once the Middle East conflict de-escalates as a result of work that oil producers need to undertake to resolve disruption, according to Barclays Plc analysts.

articles.title: Japan downgrades tsunami warning after strong earthquake
articles.content: No reports of major damage from waves that hit north-eastern coastline after magnitude 7.4 tremor

articles.title: La guerra en Medio Oriente sacude la economía global: salen datos clave y se debate el riesgo de estanflación
articles.content: Esta semana se conocerán encuestas empresariales, ventas minoristas e indicadores de precios en Estados Unidos, Europa, Asia y América Latina. El mercado buscará medir cuánto daño dejó el conflicto con Irán sobre crecimiento e inflación.

articles.title: Oil and Gas Rise After US Seizure of Iranian Ship Imperils Talks
articles.content: Oil and natural gas prices rose after the US Navy seized an Iranian ship during a chaotic weekend that saw Tehran firing at vessels and reimposing controls in the Strait of Hormuz.

articles.title: USA Rare Earth to Buy Brazilian Miner in $2.8 Billion Deal
articles.content: USA Rare Earth Inc. agreed to acquire Brazil’s Serra Verde Group in a $2.8 billion cash-and-stock transaction, marking one of the largest deals in the rare-earths industry.

articles.title: Kuwait Declares Force Majeure on Oil Shipments on Hormuz Halt
articles.content: Kuwait declared force majeure on shipments of crude oil and refined products as the blockade of the Strait of Hormuz makes it impossible to meet obligations to customers that can’t bring vessels into the Persian Gulf.

articles.title: Eni, Repsol Reach Deal to Start Exporting Venezuelan Gas in 2031
articles.content: Eni SpA and Repsol SA plan to start exporting natural gas from Venezuela by the end of 2031 after reaching a deal with Caracas to revive a long-stalled effort to expand production from a massive offshore field.

articles.title: Congo’s Debut Eurobond to Help Diversify Economy, Minister Says
articles.content: The Democratic Republic of Congo will use the proceeds of its debut bond sale to invest in hydropower and transport infrastructure, part of an effort to diversify the resource-rich Central African nation, Finance Minister Doudou Fwamba Likunde said.

articles.title: Iran Hesitant on Talks as Tensions Rise After US Seizes Ship
articles.content: Iran expressed reluctance to send diplomats to Pakistan for a second round of peace talks after the US maintained its blockade of the Strait of Hormuz and seized an Iranian ship.

articles.title: Blackstone-Backed Jersey Mike’s Submits Confidential IPO Filing
articles.content: Jersey Mike’s Subs, the sandwich chain with more than 3,000 locations, submitted a confidential filing with the US Securities and Exchange Commission for its initial public offering.

articles.title: Brazil Economists Lift Selic 2026, 2027 Forecasts on Oil Spike
articles.content: Brazil economists raised their forecasts for the benchmark interest rate for this year and next as an energy price spike from the war in Iran reverberates though Latin America’s biggest economy.

articles.content: Oil rises 5 per cent as Tehran also threatens to retaliate for US seizure of cargo ship

articles.content: The war is currently more likely to escalate than to be resolved by negotiation

articles.title: France and Germany plan ‘symbolic’ EU membership benefits for Ukraine
articles.content: Berlin and Paris say no to Kyiv accessing bloc’s budget and having voting rights until it becomes a full member

articles.title: Starmer was told to vet Mandelson before appointing him
articles.content: Revelation comes ahead of PM addressing Commons about officials’ failure to share findings of security checks

articles.title: La industria metalúrgica cayó 4,1% en marzo y la capacidad instalada tocó su nivel más bajo en cuatro años
articles.content: El sector acumula una contracción del 6,9% en el primer trimestre de 2026. ADIMRA advirtió por la falta de demanda interna, caída del empleo y márgenes cada vez más ajustados.

articles.title: ¿Chau plazo fijo? Así operan los principales bancos hoy, lunes 20 de abril
articles.content: Las tasas que ofrecen los bancos muestran cambios y reavivan la duda sobre la conveniencia del plazo fijo frente a otras opciones.

articles.title: Kevin Warsh, el candidato de Donald Trump para presidir la Fed enfrenta su primera prueba en el Congreso de EEUU
articles.content: Kevin Warsh, el nominado para suceder a Jerome Powell, deberá hacer frente a las preguntas de los legisladores norteamericanos, que indagarán sobre sus planes para la Reserva Federal.

articles.title: Hoy es el último día para aprovechar el Electro Black: como y dónde acceder al descuento
articles.content: Es la última oportunidad para aprovechar los descuentos de una de las cadenas de supermercados más importantes del país.

articles.title: Psychedelic Stocks Soar as Trump Signs Order to Ease Access
articles.content: Shares of companies involved in psychedelic-tied therapies are surging in Monday trading after President Donald Trump signed an executive order to expedite research and access.

articles.title: ECB’s Pereira Says Economic Damage of Iran War Has Yet to Show
articles.content: The Iran war is still too fresh to grasp its full impact on the euro-zone economy, according to European Central Bank Governing Council member Alvaro Santos Pereira.

articles.title: Airlines Face Massive Fare Surge: Analyst
articles.content: Airlines are heading toward consolidation as soaring jet fuel prices threaten weaker players and could push fares up as much as 30%. Meanwhile, defense spending is booming globally, but contractors face political pressure, capacity limits, and uncertain profits. Sheila Kahyaoglu, Senior Equity Research Analyst at Jefferies joined Bloomberg Open Interest to break down why the aviation and defense industries are at a critical turning point. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Citadel Saw Opportunity in Distillate Cracks Ahead of Iran War
articles.content: Distillate crack spreads were one area where Citadel saw an opportunity ahead of the Iran war, the financial giant’s head of commodities Sebastian Barrack said at an FT event in London.

articles.title: US Stocks Decline Amid Fading US-Iran Peace Talk Prospects
articles.content: US stocks fell on Monday as weekend developments in the Middle East war dimmed prospects for peace talks between the US and Iran.

articles.title: Colombia Kicks Off Third Global Bond Buyback Ahead of Elections
articles.content: Colombia is buying back some of its outstanding global bonds, its third such operation in the past year, as part of a broader strategy to reduce borrowing costs weeks ahead of a tight presidential race.

articles.title: Banks Rejected By Supreme Court in Muni Bond Price-Fixing Case
articles.content: The US Supreme Court refused to block a class action lawsuit that seeks billions of dollars from Bank of America Corp., JPMorgan Chase & Co. and other financial institutions for allegedly fixing prices in the municipal bond market.

articles.title: Stocks Halt Rally as Oil Climbs on Hormuz Setbacks: Markets Wrap
articles.content: A rally in oil drove stocks lower as a turbulent weekend in the Middle East cast doubt on prospects for peace talks between the US and Iran before their fragile ceasefire deal expires on Tuesday.

articles.title: Magyar Unveils Cabinet Team to Lead Hungary’s Economic Reset
articles.content: Hungary’s incoming Prime Minister Peter Magyar named the key ministers for his new government who will help shoulder the task of repairing frayed European ties and relaunching a stagnant economy.

articles.title: Italy Eyes Deficit Under 3% of GDP Even as Iran War Slows Growth
articles.content: Italy’s government expects the country’s budget deficit to fall below the European Union limit this year despite a downward revision of its growth forecast due to the Iran war, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Cleveland-Cliffs ‘No Longer in a Hurry’ on POSCO Deal, CEO Says
articles.content: Cleveland-Cliffs Inc.’s chief executive officer said improving US steel prices and stronger demand from the American auto industry are taking the pressure off closing a deal with South Korea’s POSCO Holdings Inc.

articles.title: BNP Paribas’ Bigos: Growth Drivers vs. Volatility
articles.content: Ecaterina Bigos, CIO for Core Investments Asia ex-Japan at BNP Paribas Asset Management, discussed the current market environment characterized by volatility and geopolitical uncertainty, they largely assume oil prices will stabilize between $80 and $90 by mid-summer, emphasized the 'structural advantage' seen in sectors benefiting from long-term growth drivers, particularly technology supported by AI-related capital expenditure, which has experienced notable rerating and is expected to sustain momentum through year-end. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Iran Hesitant on Talks Even as Trump Says Vance Is on Way
articles.content: Iran held back from saying whether it would take part in further peace talks even after US President Donald Trump said a delegation is on its way to Pakistan, creating uncertainty about whether a breakthrough can be achieved.

articles.title: Colombia Kicks Off Global Bond Buyback Ahead of Elections
articles.content: Colombia is buying back some of its outstanding global bonds, its third such operation in the past year, as part of a broader strategy to reduce borrowing costs weeks ahead of a tight presidential race.

articles.title: Starmer addresses MPs over Mandelson vetting scandal
articles.content: PM updates the Commons about officials’ failure to share findings of security checks

articles.title: DOJ Steps Up Agriculture Market Scrutiny Amid Rising Prices
articles.content: As millions of Americans face an affordability crisis, the Justice Department is homing in on the agriculture industry, focusing on the rising costs faced by farmers and ranchers and soaring prices of key grocery staples like beef and eggs.

articles.title: Cannabis CEO Calls for Reform
articles.content: Ben Kovler, the Green Thumb Industries Founder and CEO joins Bloomberg Open Interest to break down why federal rescheduling of cannabis could transform the $30B industry overnight. From blocked access to capital markets to booming consumer demand, he explains how outdated regulations are slowing growth, and why change could unlock massive opportunity, especially for research and veterans. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Braskem Majority Stake Sale by Novonor Advances With Signed Deal
articles.content: The sale of a controlling stake in Brazilian petrochemicals producer Braskem SA moved forward another step after troubled conglomerate Novonor signed an agreement to sell its shares to a fund backed by asset manager IG4 Capital.

articles.title: Secondaries Market Goes Mainstream
articles.content: Private market secondaries had a record 2025. Todd Miller of Jefferies joined Bloomberg Open Interest to explain why this once niche market is now core to institutional investing, how liquidity demand is fueling record growth, and why older private equity assets are still trading at steep discounts. A sharp look inside one of finance’s fastest-growing markets. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Stocks Fall, Oil Jumps on Doubts Over Peace Deal: Markets Wrap
articles.content: A rally in oil drove stocks lower as a turbulent weekend in the Middle East cast doubt on prospects for peace talks between the US and Iran before their fragile ceasefire deal expires.

articles.title: Bank of Canada Surveys Show War Hitting Inflation Expectations
articles.content: The war in Iran is pushing up inflation expectations, after sentiment had been improving prior to the conflict, survey data released Monday by the Bank of Canada shows.

articles.title: SEC, CFTC Propose Narrowing Hedge Fund Reporting Requirements
articles.content: Wall Street’s top regulators unveiled a plan to reduce hedge fund reporting requirements, according to a statement.

articles.title: US Stocks Drop as Iran’s Hesitancy on Peace Talks Force Pullback
articles.content: US stocks fell on Monday as weekend developments in the Middle East war dimmed prospects for peace talks between the US and Iran.

articles.title: Christine Lagarde: The energy shock: where we stand and what we need to know
Lagarde Says War’s ‘Double Uncertainty’ Means More Data Needed
articles.content: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said two main factors around the Iran war are hampering efforts to map out a monetary-policy response.

articles.title: Warsh Vows to Protect Fed’s Independence If Confirmed as Chair
articles.content: Kevin Warsh, Donald Trump’s nominee to be the next chair of the Federal Reserve, vowed to protect the US central bank’s independence if he is confirmed to the role.

articles.title: QVC Lost the Home Shoppers to Social Media Influencers
articles.content: The cable-TV seller faces bankruptcy as TikTok Shop and other video platforms take over.

articles.title: Most Emerging Currencies Slip as Middle East Uncertainty Lingers
articles.content: Most emerging-market currencies weakened Monday after Iran closed the Strait of Hormuz again and President Donald Trump sent mixed signals about progress on a peace deal with the Islamic Republic.

articles.title: HORMUZ TRACKER: Traffic Slows to Trickle as Opening Hopes Dashed
articles.content: The Strait of Hormuz now appears to be just as closed as before Iran and the US said the vital oil channel had been reopened.

articles.title: Baltic Consults on Benchmark Tanker Route as War Upends Rates
articles.content: The Baltic Exchange in London is consulting the shipping market about making changes to its benchmark tanker rate after the Iran war closed the Strait of Hormuz, rendering part of the normal trade route inaccessible.

articles.title: Who Wins from the Iran Conflict?
articles.content: Eurasia Group President and Founder Ian Bremmer joined Bloomberg Open Interest to break down the fragile reality behind the US-Iran talks. He warns that a peace deal may be more political theater than real progress. As Iran regroups during a cease-fire and oil prices reshape global power, the US faces shrinking leverage while China quietly gains ground. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Russia Sees China Buying Gas at Discount to Europe Through 2029
articles.content: Russia’s government expects to sell natural gas for several years to China at about a third less than the price paid by Europe, underscoring how a pivot to Asia hasn’t fully offset the loss of most of the country’s Western markets.

articles.title: US Stocks Decline as Trump Casts Doubt on Ceasefire Extension
articles.content: US stocks fell on Monday as President Donald Trump said an extension to the ceasefire with Iran was unlikely if a deal was not reached before Wednesday.

articles.title: IMF Draws Packed Senegal Session as New Program Talks Drag
articles.content: The International Monetary Fund’s mission chief for Senegal met a packed room of investors in Washington on Friday, offering fresh details on the country’s economic outlook but stopping short of signaling a breakthrough in talks on a new program.

articles.title: BofA Says Buy US Treasuries That Have Lagged Broader Iran Rally
articles.content: US Treasuries are still pricing in a war in the Middle East that other asset classes have shrugged off, setting up a drop in yields as they catch up, according to Bank of America.

articles.title: Eni and Repsol Plan to Start Venezuela Gas Exports in 2031
articles.content: Eni SpA and Repsol SA plan to start exporting natural gas from Venezuela by the end of 2031 after reaching a deal with Caracas to revive a long-stalled effort to expand production from a massive offshore field.

articles.title: JPMorgan, Citi on Global Payments Rivalry
articles.content: JPMorgan Chase & Co.’s global co-head of payments, Umar Farooq, and Shahmir Khaliq, global head of services at Citigroup, talk about how the banks are playing in the new global payments space. They spoke to Bloomberg’s Emily Mason in New York last month. (Corrects name of second guest to Shahmir Khaliq)

articles.title: Direct Lending Fundraising Hits Three-Year Low in First Quarter
articles.content: Direct-lending strategy funds raised a total of about $10.7 billion in funds completed during the first quarter, the lowest quarterly amount recorded in the past three years, according to data compiled by With Intelligence, an alternative investment research provider owned by S&P Global.

articles.title: Fertitta Extends Talks for $18 Billion Takeover of Caesars
articles.content: Caesars Entertainment Inc. has extended the period of exclusive talks about an $18 billion takeover by Tilman Fertitta as new details about the deal emerge.

articles.content: European Central Bank President Christine Lagarde said two main factors around the Iran war are hampering efforts to map out a monetary-policy response.

articles.title: Oil Surges After Iran Strait Closure | Open Interest 4/20/2026
articles.content: Get a jump start on the US trading day with Matt Miller and Dani Burger on "Bloomberg Open Interest."Tensions explode in the Middle East as the US seizes an Iranian ship and Tehran shuts the Strait of Hormuz, sending oil surging and markets tumbling. A volatile week ahead with earnings, the Fed, and Treasurys all in focus. Eurasia Group's Ian Bremmer on fragile US-Iran talks and China’s rising leverage. Ben Kovler on cannabis reform unlocking billions. Todd Miller of Jefferies joins us on the private markets boom. And Sheila Kahyaoglu on airlines, defense, and the pressure from soaring fuel. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.content: Bad Bunny tenía un problema. Días antes de que la megaestrella puertorriqueña se presentara en Colombia anteriormente este año, la posibilidad de fuertes lluvias amenazaba con que tres conciertos agotados en Medellín se convirtieran en pérdidas multimillonarias.

articles.title: Spirit Floats US Government Stake to Avoid Potential Liquidation
articles.content: Spirit Aviation Holdings Inc. has floated offering the US government an equity stake in the discount carrier to help stave off its potential liquidation, according to people familiar with the matter.

articles.title: Even Big Profits Yield Small Stock Pops in Market Fixated on War
articles.content: One week into the latest quarterly earnings season, even companies that are clearing Wall Street’s already high bar are seeing little in the way of stock-price jumps — another testament to how much the market is hanging on the US-Iran war.

articles.title: Warsh to Focus on Fed's Monetary Independence in Confirmation Hearing
articles.content: Bloomberg's Stuart Paul said that a preview of prepared remarks from Trump's pick for Fed Chair Kevin Warsh show that he will focus mainly on the Fed's monetary independence in his confirmation hearing Tuesday.  Paul described Warsh's view that the fed is responsible for all inflation, even if it is driven up by external forces, as 'shockingly hawkish' but explained that it could stem from the pressure he will face from the White House to deliver rate cuts.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Why Brad Jacobs' QXO Is Buying TopBuild in $17 Billion Deal
articles.content: QXO CEO Brad Jacobs explains why the company is acquiring insulation company TopBuild Corp. for about $17 billion on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Treasuries Slip as Renewed Ceasefire Tensions Lift Oil Prices
articles.content: The Treasuries market steadied at the start of the week, with bond investors alert for catalysts tied to peace talks to end the war in the Middle East and a hearing to potentially confirm Kevin Warsh as the next chair of the Federal Reserve.

articles.title: DRAM ETF Surpasses $1B Assets Since Early April Launch
articles.content: Bloomberg Intelligence's Jake Silverman joins Katie Greifeld, Scarlet Fu, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." The Roundhill Memory ETF (ticker: DRAM) has the most concentrated exposure to the three dominant memory stocks: SK Hynix, Micron, and Samsung. The ETF already has over $1 billion in assets since it's launch on April 2nd.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Romania Coalition Nears Collapse as Top Party Spurns Premier
articles.content: Romania’s government has been thrown into turmoil after the top ruling party withdrew its support for Prime Minister Ilie Bolojan, whose own bloc is now rallying to salvage his control of the four-way coalition.

articles.title: Oil and Gas Rise as Hormuz Tensions Flare Ahead of Iran Deadline
articles.content: Oil and natural gas prices jumped after renewed risks to energy flows via the Strait of Hormuz threatened to jeopardize much-anticipated peace talks between the US and Iran.

articles.title: Test Your ETF Knowledge In Our IQ Test
articles.content: Bloomberg's Joel Weber puts Scarlet Fu, Katie Greifeld, and Eric Balchunas to the test on "Bloomberg ETF IQ" in this week's edition of "IQ Test." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Sunshine Silver Weighs $400 Million IPO to Fund Reopening of Idaho Mine
articles.content: Sunshine Silver Mining & Refining Corp. is pursuing an initial public offering to help reopen an Idaho mine, according to people familiar with the matter, adding to a pipeline of mining listings as elevated metals prices draw investor interest.

articles.title: Kuwait Declares Further Force Majeure on Oil Shipments
articles.content: Kuwait declared a further force majeure on shipments of crude oil and refined products, taking into account that it would not be immediately able to meet its full obligations to customers even when the Strait of Hormuz reopens.

articles.title: Michael Baron Discusses RONB, Musk, & SpaceX
articles.content: Michael Baron, co-president and portfolio manager at Baron Capital, joins Katie Greifeld, Scarlet Fu, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss Baron Capital's entrance into the ETF space and the firm's investment in SpaceX.  (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: PRIV Owns High-Grade Private Credit: Apollo's Gosden
articles.content: Andrew Gosden, partner at Apollo Management, joins Katie Greifeld, Scarlet Fu, and Eric Balchunas on "Bloomberg ETF IQ." They discuss the State Street IG Public & Private Credit ETF (ticker: PRIV). The fund has amassed more than $800 million in assets since its launch in February of 2025. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Emerging Currencies Trade Mixed With Middle East War in Focus
articles.content: Emerging-market currencies traded mixed on Monday, with global risk appetite wobbling as investors monitored the prospects for the US and Iran to agree on a deal to end the war.

articles.title: South America Could Unlock 2.1 Million Barrels a Day on $100 Oil
articles.content: South America could deliver an additional 2.1 million barrels of daily oil supplies by 2035 with crude prices around $100, Rystad Energy wrote in note on Monday.

articles.title: US Stocks Halt Five-Day Rally as Ceasefire Extension In Doubt
articles.content: US stocks slipped on Monday as President Donald Trump said an extension to the ceasefire with Iran was unlikely.

articles.title: Diplo, Twenty One Pilots Royalties to Back Bonds Sold by Chord
articles.content: Chord Music Partners, a firm which invests in music rights, is selling $500 million of bonds backed by royalties from artists including Twenty One Pilots, Diplo and Morgan Wallen.

articles.title: Stock Market’s Rally to Record High Masks Weak Building Blocks
articles.content: The record-breaking advance in US stocks has many of the hallmarks of an unloved rally, including narrow leadership, low volumes and muted investor sentiment.

articles.title: EQB Inc. Sells C$200M of Limited Recourse Capital Notes
articles.content: Canada’s EQB Inc., a bank holding company, sold C$200 million ($146 million) of additional tier one securities on Monday, according to people with knowledge of the matter and confirming an earlier Bloomberg report.

articles.title: Cuánto debés invertir en un plazo fijo de 30 días para ganar $30.000
articles.content: El rendimiento varía según la tasa del banco y si la operación se realiza por sucursal o de forma electrónica: conocé los detalles.

articles.title: Compass Pathways CEO on Trump's Order to Expand Psychedelics Research
articles.content: Shares of companies involved in psychedelic-tied therapies surged Monday after President Donald Trump signed an executive order to expedite research and access. US-traded shares of Compass Pathways spiked as much as 53% while AtaiBeckley surged by 37%. GH Research jumped 34% and Definium Therapeutics rallied 16%. The AdvisorShares Psychedelics ETF (PSIL) also climbed as much as 20% — touching its highest intraday level since July 2023. The new order directs the US Food and Drug Administration to issue expedited review vouchers to psychedelics with a so-called breakthrough designation, shortening the review time to one to two months from six to ten months. AtaiBeckley, Definium and Compass Pathways all have therapies with breakthrough status. Compass Pathways CEO Kabir Nath joins Bloomberg Businessweek Daily to discuss. He speaks with Carol Massar and Tim Stenovec. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Agnico Expands in Finland With C$3.7 Billion Gold Deal Trio
articles.content: Agnico Eagle Mines Ltd. plans to spend C$3.7 billion ($2.7 billion) to acquire three gold projects in northern Finland, expanding the Canadian miner’s footprint in the Scandinavian country.

articles.title: FDA's Makary on Trump EO to Ease Access to Psychedelics
articles.content: FDA Commissioner Dr. Marty Makary discusses President Trump's Executive Order aimed at faster research and access to psychedelics, citing promising research and calling this an 'historic moment.' He talks with Romaine Bostick and Katie Greifeld on "The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Caesars Extends Talks for $18 Billion Takeover by Golden Nugget Owner
articles.content: Caesars Entertainment Inc. has extended a period of exclusive talks about an $18 billion takeover by Tilman Fertitta as new details about the deal emerge.

articles.title: BMO's McCormick Says It's Still Right to Be Long Dollar
articles.content: BMO Chief FX Strategist Mark McCormick says the right trade is to be long dollar for now. He speaks on "Bloomberg The Close." (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.content: Oil slipped on signs that Iran will attend negotiations with the US in Islamabad before a ceasefire between the sides ends.

articles.title: Stocks Set for Gains, Oil Dips on Peace Deal Hopes: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Oil slipped and US equity futures edged higher as signals that Iran may join talks with Washington in Islamabad raised hopes for progress before a looming ceasefire deadline.

articles.title: Gold Steadies as Traders Weigh Next Round of US-Iran Peace Talks
articles.content: Gold steadied, as traders weighed prospects for a negotiated settlement to the war in Iran that’s disrupted global energy supplies and heightened inflation risks.

articles.content: Rio Tinto Group’s production of iron ore from Australia rose in the first quarter, while seasonal weather disruptions shuttered ports in March.

articles.title: Steel Dynamics Revenue Grows Most Since 2022, Beating Estimates
articles.content: Steel Dynamics Inc. beat analysts’ estimates for first-quarter revenue, reporting its fastest growth in nearly four years as rising steel prices and a record 3.6 million tons in steel shipments drove a rebound in profit.

articles.content: La caída del empleo formal y el avance del monotributo deterioran el financiamiento del sistema, que depende cada vez más de transferencias estatales y pierde poder adquisitivo.

articles.title: ATE realizará un paro nacional por reclamo salarial y no descarta bloquear los accesos a aeropuertos
articles.content: El gremio de estatales se concentrará este martes en diferentes puntos del país, con epicentro en las inmediaciones del Aeroparque Jorge Newbery. Aseguran que los trabajadores públicos perdieron más de $11 millones en dos años de gobierno de Javier Milei.

articles.title: Asian Stocks Gain, Oil Dips on Hopes of Iran Talks: Markets Wrap
articles.content: Asian stocks edged higher as signs Iran may join talks with the US fostered cautious optimism about progress in the Middle East ahead of a looming ceasefire deadline. Oil dropped.

articles.title: Apple Names Ternus as Next CEO, Cook to Become Chairman
articles.content: Apple has named hardware chief John Ternus as its next leader, with Tim Cook shifting to the role of executive chairman. Ternus will become CEO on September 1. Ed Ludlow has more. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: South Korean Stock Index Rises to Record as AI Optimism Extends
articles.content: South Korean stocks climbed to a record, powered by chipmakers as the artificial intelligence trade regained momentum and optimism reemerged about a US-Iran peace deal.

articles.title: 'Time Is On Iran's Side': Defense Priorities' Kelanic
articles.content: Defense Priorities’ Rosemary Kelanic tells Bloomberg TV that Iran believes prolonging negotiations will yield more leverage. She notes that while both sides want a deal, Tehran is in no rush to settle. (Source: Bloomberg)

articles.title: Rio Tinto Copper Production Rises as Oyu Tolgoi Ramps Up
articles.content: Rio Tinto Group’s copper production rose 9% in the first quarter, thanks to a ramp-up at the Oyu Tolgoi copper mine in Mongolia, while iron ore output in Australia also climbed in spite of seasonal weather disruptions.

articles.title: EQT Raises Record $15.6 Billion for Asia Private Equity Fund
articles.content: EQT AB raised $15.6 billion for its latest private equity Asia fund, the largest pool of capital ever assembled for the region, as global investors look beyond the US amid elevated uncertainty.

articles.title: MSCI Delays High-Stakes Indonesia Review After Downgrade Fears Spooked Investors
articles.content: MSCI Inc. will maintain a string of measures on Indonesian equities and provide an update on the nation’s market status in June, saying that it’s evaluating the reforms rolled out by regulators.

articles.title: Victory Giant Surges in Debut After Biggest Hong Kong Listing This Year
articles.content: Victory Giant Technology Huizhou Co., a Chinese supplier of Nvidia Corp., surged 57% in its Hong Kong trading debut after raising $2.6 billion in the city’s largest listing in seven months.

articles.title: MTR Markets First Public HK Dollar Bond, Adding to Rush of Deals
articles.content: MTR Corp Ltd. began marketing its first-ever Hong Kong dollar public bond offering Tuesday, adding to an uptick of borrowing in the local currency debt market.

articles.title: China’s Aluminum Output Fueled by Middle East’s Stranded Cargoes
articles.content: Chinese imports of the main raw material for aluminum jumped to a two-year high in March, as cargoes bound for smelters in the Middle East were rerouted to the world’s biggest producer.
